description_long: This is a two-story suburban house with a prominent dark gable roof and light-colored exterior walls, likely finished in siding or painted brick. The front facade features a central bay window section flanked by large rectangular windows, and a covered porch supported by a single column. The house has multiple window arrangements, including a row of three windows on the front bay and a mix of single and double windows on the sides and rear. The roof is steeply pitched with intersecting gables, and a single chimney is visible. The rear of the house includes a secondary covered entryway and a modest back porch, while the overall structure sits on a raised concrete foundation.
description: A two-story suburban house with a dark gable roof, light exterior walls, bay windows, and a covered front porch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry with a covered porch, central bay windows, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the gable end, single entry door, and a mix of window sizes.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a secondary covered entry, fewer windows, and the back porch area.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the gable end, bay window extension, and porch support column.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered front porch with a single column, overhanging roof, and central door flanked by large windows
